name: Rufinamide
description: Rufinamide is a triazole derivative and an anticonvulsant medication to treat seizure disorders like Lennox-Gastuat syndrome, a form of childhood epilepsy. Clinical trials suggest its efficacy in the treatment of partial seizures.
indication: Adjunct therapy for treatment of seizures associated with Lennox-Gastaut syndrome.
toxicity: The most commonly observed adverse reactions (≥10% and greater than placebo) were headache, dizziness, fatigue, somnolence, and nausea.
mechanismOfAction: Rufinamide is a triazole derivative antiepileptic that prolongs the inactive state of voltage gated sodium channels thus stabilizing membranes, ultimately blocking the spread of partial seizure activity.
pharmacodynamics: At high concentrations will inhibit action of mGluR5 subtype receptors thus preventing the production of glutamate.

interactions.name: Rilpivirine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Rilpivirine can be decreased when it is combined with Rufinamide.

interactions.name: Mefloquine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Rufinamide can be decreased when used in combination with Mefloquine.
